reasoning: Geometry check: (1) Column 0 vs column 31 — both show green-dominant pixels with scattered tan fragments; no hard seam line visible at the tile boundary in the 4x repeated panels. (2) Row 0 vs row 31 — top and bottom edges are similarly green-dominant; continuity is acceptable with no obvious horizontal stripe artifact. (3) 3x3 mental tile test — the larger golden/tan stone blob clusters (~8-10px wide aggregates) repeat at consistent offsets and do draw mild attention as a semi-regular polka-dot rhythm, though the green fill dilutes this effect. Overall: edge continuity passes the seam test; no single dominant bright hotspot, but the tan stone patches are large enough and irregular enough in distribution that they create a slightly uneven density — upper-center area of the tile has noticeably denser golden coverage compared to lower-left which is more uniformly green. This density imbalance will be visible on a large tiled ground. The texture serves its 'outdoor ruins ground' role adequately and will not strongly compete with foreground sprites due to the muted earthy palette, but the stone cluster repetition and density variance prevent a clean pass.
